mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden jump in the unemployment rate above 4.5 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in 70 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5 percent, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative mean 60-day returns, or the interaction term loses explanatory power in a multivariate regression over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and rising corporate output, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 20-day returns during recovery periods, or the predictive coefficient turns negative with p < 0.05 in a regime-filtered sample.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls confirms economic resilience and supports wage-driven consumption, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth correlates with negative 5-day forward returns, or the Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained CPIAUCSL readings above 253.0 indicate accelerating price pressures that force central banks to tighten policy, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL exceeds 253.0, or the signal out-of-sample R-squared drops below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms synchronized demand growth across production and consumption channels, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1 and RSAFS expansion signal yields negative mean 20-day returns, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals manufacturing weakness and reduced corporate earnings expectations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns in late-cycle periods, or the predictive accuracy drops below 50 percent in a regime-filtered out-of-sample test.
